description: Lineup-Aware Team Generation - replace selectable fitness algorithms with one canonical lineup-aware fitness path that preserves existing bulk/safety/team-quality optimization, scores PlayPokemon bring-6 rosters by ordered pick-3 lineups, and controls runtime with staged cached scoring.

userStories.title: Add lineup-aware generation types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed lineup, roster metric, bench utility, and recommendation structures so that lineup-aware fitness can be implemented safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add types for ordered lineup roles: lead, switch, and closer
Add types for recommended lineup output including score, coverage metrics, covered threats, weaknesses, ABC/ABB/ABA diagnostic label, and optional resource path metrics

Add types for PlayPokemon roster metrics including viable lineup count, top lineup quality, top-N lineup depth, dominating matchup rate, overwhelming loss rate, single-answer risks, viable lead diversity, and bench utility summary
Add types for bench utility including utility score, total appearances, lead appearances, switch appearances, closer appearances, and warnings
Add types or configuration structures that support fast fitness scoring separately from full UI-ready diagnostics
Do not expose FitnessAlgorithm as a user-selectable API or UI option in new types
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement PlayPokemon lineup enumeration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deterministic lineup enumeration so that every bring-6 roster is scored against the same 60 ordered pick-3 options.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a pure lineup enumeration module under lib/genetic/fitness
Given six unique Pokemon, enumeration returns exactly 60 lineups
Enumeration includes exactly six distinct leads
Each lead has exactly ten canonical unordered backline pairs
Backline pair order is canonicalized and duplicate backline permutations are not returned
Lead identity is part of lineup identity, so the same three Pokemon with a different lead is a distinct lineup
Enumeration throws or returns a typed error for non-six-Pokemon PlayPokemon roster input
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement lineup scoring helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure lineup scoring helpers so that individual ordered pick-3 lineups can be evaluated consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a pure lineup scoring module under lib/genetic/fitness
Audit existing fitness factors in individual.ts and teamSynergy.ts before removing or retiring old scoring code

Lineup score preserves retained existing quality signals for ranking quality, bulk/stat balance, role strength, type synergy, type diversity, move coverage, energy pressure, simulation coverage, single-counter risk, core-breaker risk, and shield scenario reliability where data exists
Lineup score accounts for lead performance against threats
Lineup score accounts for backline coverage and shared weaknesses
Lineup score accounts for safe-swap quality for the switch role
Lineup score accounts for closer or no-shield strength for the closer role
Lineup score accounts for threat coverage and single-answer risks
Dominating matchup count uses battle rating greater than 600
Overwhelming loss count uses battle rating less than 400
ABC/ABB/ABA label is returned as diagnostics and is not used as a primary scoring input
Lead-specific matchup data can change the score for the same three Pokemon
At least one test proves a retained bulk, safety, or equivalent team-quality signal can affect lineup score
Any current fitness factor that is intentionally not migrated is documented in code comments or test notes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add shield and resource path scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shield-path scoring so that lineups use shield-specific matchup data when available without treating missing data as losses.
acceptanceCriteria: Balanced path uses lead 1-shield data and backline 1-shield data when available
Shield-spend path uses lead 2-shield data and backline 0-shield data when available
Shield-save path uses lead 0-shield data and backline 2-shield data when available
Missing shield-specific matchup data is marked unavailable or falls back neutrally
Missing shield-specific matchup data is not counted as an automatic loss
Resource path metrics are omitted or marked unavailable when there is insufficient data to compute them safely
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement PlayPokemon roster scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need roster-level fitness aggregation so that bring-6 teams are rewarded for lineup depth instead of one isolated best lineup.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a pure roster scoring module under lib/genetic/fitness
Roster scoring evaluates exactly 60 enumerated lineups for each six-Pokemon roster
Roster scoring can use a fast scoring mode for hot GA evaluation and a full scoring mode for finalist or final output diagnostics
Roster scoring rewards multiple viable lineups
Roster scoring rewards strong top lineup quality and top-N lineup depth
Roster scoring rewards viable lead diversity
Roster scoring rewards broad threat coverage and useful bench Pokemon
Roster scoring penalizes one excellent lineup with a dead or unbringable bench
Roster scoring penalizes single-answer dependency, repeated shared weaknesses, poor lead diversity, and high overwhelming-loss rate
Dominating and overwhelming metrics are calculated at lineup level before roster aggregation
Tests cover that staged scoring avoids requiring full UI-ready diagnostics for every roster evaluation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement lineup recommendations and bench utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need recommendation and bench utility helpers so that generated rosters include actionable pick-3 plans and warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add recommendation helpers under lib/genetic/fitness
PlayPokemon recommendation output includes the top five recommended pick-3 lineups by default
Each recommended lineup includes lead, switch, closer, score, coverage metrics, covered threats, weaknesses, ABC/ABB/ABA diagnostic label, and resource path metrics when available
Full resource path details and explanation-heavy diagnostics are built only for recommended or finalist lineups, not every chromosome in every generation
Bench utility is calculated for every roster member
Bench utility includes utility score, total recommended lineup appearances, lead appearances, switch appearances, and closer appearances
Pokemon with zero recommended lineup appearances receive an unbringable warning
Pokemon below the low-utility threshold receive a low-utility warning
API-ready recommendation output does not return all 60 lineups by default
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

Per-run caches are used for repeated matchup, shield matchup, role score, lineup score, or roster score calculations in the GA evaluation path
Hot GA scoring uses bounded deterministic threat pools rather than unbounded full-meta sweeps for every chromosome
Remove backend branching between individual and teamSynergy fitness paths
PlayPokemon GA fitness uses roster scoring for six-Pokemon rosters
GBL GA fitness preserves genetic fitness selection while using role-ordered lineup scoring for three-Pokemon teams
The algorithm no longer accepts an algorithm selection parameter for fitness routing
Full UI-ready lineup diagnostics are computed for the final selected team or bounded finalists, not for every chromosome
Existing generation tests are updated to assert lineup-aware output

userStories.title: Add normalized weighted score component contract
userStories.description: As an optimizer developer, I need a normalized weighted score contract so lineup and roster scoring combine documented metrics consistently. Ralph must read or invoke the gbl-optimizer skill before optimizer scoring work.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph reads or invokes the gbl-optimizer skill before changing scoring contracts
Ralph records the optimizer docs read in progress.txt for this iteration
Add a score breakdown type or module with normalized components: synergy, coverage, safety, consistency, bulk, defensiveRatio, offensiveRatio, and role
Each component is bounded to 0..1 before weighted aggregation
Starting weights are synergy 0.24, coverage 0.21, safety 0.17, consistency 0.13, bulk 0.10, defensiveRatio 0.07, offensiveRatio 0.05, and role 0.03
Hard constraints remain limited to legality and validity checks
Tests prove lower-priority categories can influence close outcomes without dominating higher-priority categories
Tests prove role score alone cannot dominate the final score
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added normalized weighted optimizer score breakdown contract with documented component weights, hard-constraint categories, bounded aggregation, and regression tests for lower-priority influence and role-score limits.

userStories.title: Separate top-threat and full-meta threat pools
userStories.description: As a competitive player, I need coverage scoring to distinguish common top threats from broader full-meta robustness so generated teams do not hide severe meta weaknesses. Ralph must read or invoke the gbl-optimizer skill before coverage scoring work.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph reads or invokes the gbl-optimizer skill before changing threat-pool or coverage scoring code
Ralph records the optimizer docs read in progress.txt for this iteration
Threat-pool construction produces separate top-threat and full-meta pools
Top-threat coverage is weighted higher than full-meta coverage
Coverage diagnostics expose top-threat and full-meta results separately
No-answer and single-answer threat counts are computed for both pools where data exists
A lineup that loses hard to a top threat scores lower than a lineup with only rare full-meta holes when other signals are comparable
Threat pools are bounded and deterministic for hot GA scoring paths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added bounded canonicalized separate topThreats/fullMetaThreats scoring context pools, exposed topThreatCoverage and fullMetaCoverage diagnostics on lineup coverage metrics, and weighted evaluated top-threat coverage above full-meta coverage in full and fast lineup scoring. Focused fitness tests, typecheck, lint, and full test suite pass.

userStories.title: Implement type-effectiveness offensive and defensive ratios

Type-effectiveness calculations use data/type-effectiveness.json as the source of truth
Dual-type defenders multiply both defender-type effectiveness values
Pokemon GO immunity-style interactions use 0.39x, not zero damage
Offensive ratio scoring evaluates selected fast and charged move types into top-threat and full-meta defenders
Defensive ratio scoring evaluates weaknesses, resistances, double weaknesses, double resistances, and shared weaknesses
Ratio scores are normalized to 0..1 before weighted aggregation
Tests cover dual-type multiplication, immunity-style interactions, neutral cancellation, double resistance, and double super effectiveness
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement safety consistency and bulk components
userStories.description: As a competitive player, I need the scorer to reward reliable teams that avoid catastrophic losses, volatile bait dependence, and brittle low-bulk compositions. Ralph must read or invoke the gbl-optimizer skill before safety, consistency, or bulk scoring work.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph reads or invokes the gbl-optimizer skill before changing safety, consistency, or bulk scoring
Ralph records the optimizer docs read in progress.txt for this iteration
Safety scoring accounts for overwhelming losses, no-answer threats, single-answer threats, bad-lead recovery, and sweep risk
Overwhelming losses against the top-threat pool are weighted more heavily than rare full-meta losses
Consistency scoring uses PvPoke consistency ranking data when available
Consistency scoring falls back to shield stability, damage per energy, energy cost, useful neutral damage, second-move value, and bait-dependence proxies when consistency rankings are unavailable
Bulk scoring uses normalized defense * hp / attack when direct bulk data is unavailable
Safety, consistency, and bulk are separate normalized components before aggregation
Tests cover poor bulk, bait-dependent volatility, shield-scenario instability, and no-answer or single-answer fragility
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate weighted lineup and roster aggregation

userStories.notes: Completed weighted lineup and roster aggregation integration: ordered lineup scores and fast roster lineup scores use the normalized score-breakdown contract, structure safety combines ABB-style support and ABA fragility independently, and offensive/defensive ratio components use top-threat/full-meta type-effectiveness helpers. Focused fitness tests, typecheck, lint, and full test suite pass.
